seoTitle: How to Use Brand Kit in Canva Easily
metaDescription: Set up Canva's Brand Kit with your colors, fonts, and logos so every design you create stays visually consistent with your brand.
intro: Keeping every design on-brand gets tedious if you're manually picking the same colors and fonts each time. Canva's Brand Kit stores those assets in one place so you can apply them consistently across projects. This guide walks through setting it up and using it, aimed at business owners, marketers, and content creators who want a faster path to cohesive branding.

steps.heading: Set up your Brand Kit
steps.body: Add your brand colors and fonts into the Brand Kit section so they're saved for future use.

steps.heading: Apply your brand colors
steps.body: Pull your saved colors directly into any design instead of manually matching hex codes each time.

steps.heading: Incorporate your brand fonts
steps.body: Swap in your saved fonts to keep typography consistent across documents and graphics.

steps.heading: Reuse across projects
steps.body: Apply the same Brand Kit to new designs so your branding stays consistent as your content library grows.

faq.question: How many brand colors can I save in Brand Kit?
faq.answer: You can save a set of brand colors, though the exact limit depends on your Canva plan.

faq.question: Is Brand Kit only for businesses with a logo?
faq.answer: No — even solo creators or small teams can use it just to keep colors and fonts consistent, with or without a logo.

faq.question: Does Brand Kit update designs I already made?
faq.answer: No, it applies to designs going forward; older designs need to be manually updated if you want them to match.
